rows.name: Hall Effect/Rapid Trigger
rows.note: Sub-$50 HE boards now common and most 2026 gaming keyboard launches ship Hall Effect/Rapid Trigger as baseline, per KeebFinder and PC Gamer roundups this week.

rows.name: Keychron
rows.note: V6 Ultra HE Kickstarter VIP reservation window (through July 21, 2026), the new 14-in-1 Thunderbolt 5 dock (launched July 6), and K5 Ultra 8K keep Keychron's release cadence at a brand-defining pace.

rows.name: Split/Ergo
rows.note: Kickstarter split-ergo field stayed crowded this week: TAIKO-01 physician-designed split posted a July 2026 update alongside NocFree's HK$2.1M-raised low-profile split campaign.

rows.name: HMX Cloud V2
rows.note: No new SKUs or reviews surfaced this week; Cloud V2 remains a steady catalog staple as attention shifts toward Gateron Lanes and newer linears.

rows.name: Work Louder x OpenAI Codex Micro
rows.note: OpenAI's first branded hardware, Codex Micro (built by Work Louder off the $199 Creator Micro 2), ships July 15, 2026; pre-launch teasers drove a discussion spike this week.

rows.name: Mode Designs
rows.note: Mode's Summer 2026 update: Sonnet/Encore return to in-stock after preorder fulfillment, Envoy gets a final-stock notice, and the Prologue 98% is teased for late summer.

rows.name: 75% Layout
rows.note: Buyer guides this week peg 75% at roughly 42% of compact-layout preference for 2026, with rotary-knob boards cementing it as the default first custom build.

rows.name: Alice layout
rows.note: Coverage now frames Alice as fully mainstream ('no longer exotic') with sub-$100 budget clones saturating the category, cooling novelty-driven hobbyist discussion.

rows.name: Gateron Oil King
rows.note: Four-switch Oil King lineup (linear, silent linear, silent tactile, heavy tactile) holds flagship status in Gateron's 2026 catalog; no new SKUs announced this week.

rows.name: Gateron Lanes
rows.note: ThereminGoat published a fresh Gateron Lanes switch review this week, reviving community discussion after a quiet stretch.

rows.name: GMK CYL Masterpiece R2
rows.note: No group-buy activity tracked this week; hobbyist attention has shifted to newer GMK CYL interest checks like OG Extensions and Classic.

rows.name: GMK CYL Pandemonium
rows.note: Set remains in the post-GB fulfillment queue with no fresh updates; community focus has moved to newly opened 2026 CYL interest checks.

rows.name: DCS Dolch
rows.note: Overshadowed by concurrent DCS group buys - Handarbeit (closes July 19) and Mudbeam (closed July 16) - pulling DCS-profile attention elsewhere this week.

rows.name: Prototypist
rows.note: Hosting the KeyBoy Advance group buy (Game Boy Advance-shaped 50%, closes July 22, 2026) and teasing the Paper 65 kit, reversing last week's decline.

rows.name: Wuque Studio
rows.note: Confirmed as Haimu's sole business agent outside mainland China this week; teased VANY Alice group buy and Mammoth V2/Promise V2 development stabilized sentiment.

rows.name: Cherry MX2A
rows.note: No new SKUs since April's MX2A Dummy release; buzz keeps ceding ground to magnetic/TMR switches with little fresh MX2A-specific discussion this week.

rows.name: Cherry (GMK parent)
rows.note: Cherry XTRFY's July 3, 2026 TMR-based K5 Pro Compact launch gave the Cherry brand a rare positive headline after months of MX2A-driven decline.

rows.name: GMK CYL OG Extensions
rows.note: New "[IC] GMK CYL OG Extensions" interest check posted on Geekhack with a GB window of July 15-Aug 12, 2026, generating fresh keycap-community buzz.
